must_detect.matched_text:
Northwick Capital plc is a UK‑listed financial services issuer; this Employee Privacy and MNPI Handling Notice applies to staff and contractors.
We process personal data under UK GDPR (information relating to an identified or identifiable person), including names, work emails, trade approvals, device IDs, and access logs.
Lawful bases include contract, legal obligation (market‑abuse, listing and transparency rules), and legitimate interests in compliance monitoring; DPO: Mr. Edwin Mallory, dpo@northwickcap.co.uk.
Inside information under UK MAR means precise, non‑public matters that, if made public, would be likely to have a significant effect on Northwick Capital plc’s share price.
Examples include draft half‑year results, unannounced board succession, and proposed placing terms; information already released via our RNS on 12 March 2026 (“Cloud Platform Update”, Ref: NWCP/2026/0312) is public.
If inside information is selectively disclosed to any analyst, investor, or journalist, notify the Company Secretary immediately; an announcement must be made as soon as possible, and an insider list update maintained.
Closed periods for PDMRs run for 30 calendar days before publication of year‑end and half‑year results; dealing in company securities requires pre‑clearance via compliance@northwickcap.co.uk.
We retain insider lists and delay‑of‑disclosure rationales for at least five years (UK MAR Art 18/17), and pre‑clearance records for six years from decision.
Personal data may be transferred to processors in the EEA and elsewhere; where outside the UK, we use the UK IDTA or the UK Addendum to SCCs with vendors such as Tallow Data Services Ltd.
You may submit a data subject access request to privacy@northwickcap.co.uk; we will verify identity and respond within one month, extendable by two months for complexity.
Calendar invites or file metadata referencing “Project HARRIER” with tentative pricing grids constitute MNPI even if the invite body is vague; use approved secure folders and do not email externals.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
